text: Joint Maritime Information Center says oil tankers and other vessels can use the southern (Omani) shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz with “AIS on, radars radiating, running lights on, and normal use of VHF” either at night or day. https://t.co/f7Yyv9SIs5

Iran isn’t losing any time re-starting its oil production and fresh exports (📷 via @CopernicusEU Sentinel-2 🛰️ from earlier today, showing multiple super tankers loading at Iran’s Kharg Island terminal) https://t.co/agqZcLzA5H

Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is now closed. The US says it remains open and that 17 million barrels of oil crossed the waterway today (that’s higher than pre-war, and a lot higher when the bypass pipelines volumes are added).
Iranian negotiators heading to Switzerland. US VP JD Vance expected to travel too later today.
quoted_text: The #Iran's negotiating team, known as #Minab168, left for Zurich. @mb_ghalibaf and @araghchi are leading the team.
